usage: add_via_definition [-help] {[-via_rule <via_def_rule> | -cut_pattern <viaDef>] [-name <name>]} [[[-via_rule <via_def_rule> [-cut_size {x y}]]| -cut_pattern <viaDef>] [[-cut_spacing {x y}] [-top_enclosure {x y}] [-bottom_enclosure {x y}] [-top_offset {x y}] [-bottom_offset {x y}] [-row_col {x y}] [-origin {x y}] [-pattern <pattern>]]] [-name <name> {-cut_layer <layer> -cut_rects {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...}} {[-top_layer <layer>] {-top_rects {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} | -top_polygon {{x1 y1} {x2 y2}...}}} {[-bottom_layer <layer>] {-bottom_rects {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} | -bottom_polygon {{x1 y1} {x2 y2}...}}}] [[[-via_rule <via_def_rule>] [-cut_size {x y}] [-cut_spacing {x y}] [-top_enclosure {x y}] [-bottom_enclosure {x y}] [-top_offset {x y}] [-bottom_offset {x y}] [-row_col {x y}] [-origin {x y}] [-pattern <pattern>]] | [[-cut_layer <layer>] [-cut_rects {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...}] [-top_layer <layer>] [-top_rects {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...}] [-top_polygon {{x1 y1} {x2 y2}...}] [-bottom_layer <layer>] [-bottom_rects {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...}] [-bottom_polygon {{x1 y1} {x2 y2}...}]]] [-cut_rects {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} [-cut_masks <int+>]] [-bottom_rects {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} [-bottom_layer_masks <int+>]] [-top_rects {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} [-top_layer_masks <int+>]] [-via_rule <via_def_rule> | [[-cut_masks <int+>] [-bottom_layer_masks <int+>] [-top_layer_masks <int+>]]] [-via_rule <via_def_rule> ]

options.name: -help
options.description: Prints out the command usage

options.name: -bottom_enclosure
options.description: Requires -via_rule or -cut_pattern option for a parameterized via. Specifies the bottom layer enclosure around the cut array. The enclosure measures the distance from the cut-array-edge to the metal-edge that encloses the cut-array. Values are specified in microns. Default = derived from -via_rule.

options.name: -bottom_layer
options.description: Requires -name option for a fixed via. Specifies the name or pointer of the bottom layer. If not specified the first layer below the -cut_layer will be assumed. ((layer), optional)

options.name: -bottom_layer_masks
options.description: Specifies the masks for the rects on the bottom layer for a via_def. The mask values and rects must be in the same order and there must be the same number of mask values as rects. The bottom layer mask cannot be specified for parameterized vias.

options.name: -bottom_offset
options.description: Requires -via_rule or -cut_pattern option for a parameterized via. Specifies the x,y offset for bottom layer in the via. After the non-shifted via is computed, the bottom layer rectangle is offset by adding the x,y to the -origin offset. Values are specified in microns. Default = {0 0}.

options.name: -bottom_polygon
options.description: Requires -name option for a fixed via. The polygon to create on the bottom layer. Values are specified in microns.

options.name: -bottom_rects
options.description: {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} Requires -name option for a fixed via. The list of rectangles to create on the bottom layer. Values are specified in microns.

options.name: -cut_layer
options.description: Requires -name option for a fixed via. Specifies the name or pointer of the cut layer. The top/bottom layers of the via will be derived from the routing layers above/below the cut layer or specified with -top_layer/-bottom_layer. ((layer), optional)

options.name: -cut_masks
options.description: Specifies the masks for the rects on the cut layer for a via_def. The mask values and rects must be in the same order and there must be the same number of mask values as rects. A mask pattern cannot be specified for parameterized via cuts. For parameterized via cuts, only three types of mask color patterns are allowed, checker-board (the default), alternating columns, and alternating rows using the LEF VIA PATTERN statement. Refer to the LEF VIA docs for more details.

options.name: -cut_pattern
options.description: Specifies a via, new via's cut rects are parameterized from it. ((viaDef), optional)

options.name: -cut_rects
options.description: {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} Requires -name option for a fixed via. The list of rectangles to create on the cut layer. Values are specified in microns.

options.name: -cut_size
options.description: Requires -via_rule option for a parameterized via. Specifies the size (x=width, y=height) of the cut rectangles. Values are specified in microns and must be greater than 0. Default = derived from -via_rule.

options.name: -cut_spacing
options.description: Requires -via_rule or -cut_pattern option. The spacing between the cuts. The spacing is measured from cut-edge to the next cut-edge. Values are specified in microns and must be greater than 0. Default = derived from -via_rule.

options.name: -name
options.description: Specifies the via name. The name cannot collide with any existing via-master names.

options.name: -origin
options.description: Requires -via_rule option for -cut_pattern for a parameterized via.. The x,y offset for all shapes in the via. By default the 0,0 origin of the via is the center of the cut-array and the center of the enclosing metal rectangles. After the non-shifted via is computed, all the cut and metal rectangles are offset by adding these values. size (x=width, y=height) of the cut rectangles. Values are specified in microns. Default = {0 0}

options.name: -pattern
options.description: Requires -via_rule option for a parameterized via. Specified the cut pattern encoded as an ASCII string. This parameter is only required when some of the cuts are missing from the array of cuts. Refer to the LEF/DEF Reference DEF VIA syntax for examples.

options.name: -row_col
options.description: Requires -via_rule or -cut_pattern option for a parameterized via. The number of cut rows and columns to create. Values must be greater than 0. Default = {1 1}

options.name: -top_enclosure
options.description: Requires -via_rule or -cut_pattern option for a parameterized via. The top layer enclosure around the cut array. The enclosure measures the distance from the cut-array-edge to the metal-edge that encloses the cut-array. Values are specified in microns.Default = derived from -via_rule.

options.name: -top_layer
options.description: Requires -name option for a fixed via. Specifies the name or pointer of the top layer. If not specified the first layer above the -cut_layer will be assumed. ((layer), optional)

options.name: -top_layer_masks
options.description: Specifies the masks for the rects on the top layer for a via_def. The mask values and rects must be in the same order and there must be the same number of mask values as rects. The top layer mask cannot be specified for parameterized vias.

options.name: -top_offset
options.description: Requires -via_rule or -cut_pattern option for a parameterized via. The x,y offset for top layer in the via. After the non-shifted via is computed, the top layer rectangle is offset by adding the x,y to the -origin offset. Values are specified in microns. Default = {0 0}

options.name: -top_polygon
options.description: Requires -name option for a fixed via. The polygon to create on the top layer. Values are specified in microns.

options.name: -top_rects
options.description: {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} Requires -name option for a fixed via. The list of rectangles to create on the top layer. Values are specified in microns.

options.name: -via_rule
options.description: The name or pointer of the via-rule (from LEF VIARULE GENERATE or OpenAccess standardViaDef) to use for the via master being created. Layers and default values are derived from the specified <via_rule>. When using -via_rule, a parameterized via master will be created. ((viaRuleGenerate), optional)
